articles.title: Iran promises war beyond the Middle East if Trump decides to attack
articles.summary: Iran promises war beyond the Middle East if Trump decides to attack Iran vowed to extend the war "beyond the region" of the Middle East if the US attacks it again, following threats from Donald Trump to resume hostilities if peace negotiations fail to produce a breakthrough. "If the aggression against Iran is repeated, the promised regional war will this time extend far beyond the region, and our devastating blows will crush you," declared the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps on Wednesday.

articles.title: Israel considered installing Mahmoud Ahmadinejad as ruler during war
articles.summary: Israel considered installing Mahmoud Ahmadinejad as ruler during war Israel considered installing former Iranian President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad as ruler during the early days of the US-Israeli war on the country. US officials told the New York Times that the former president, previously known for his staunchly anti-US and anti-Israel views, was directly consulted on the issue. They said Ahmadinejad was injured on the war’s first day by an Israeli strike at his home in Tehran that had been designed to free him from house arrest. "All 430 activists have been transferred to Israeli vessels and are making their way to Israel, where they will be able to meet with their consular representatives." The report said the troops were injured when a drone launched by Hezbollah struck their position. The Israeli Home Front Command also said sirens were activated in Malkiya and other areas of the Upper Galilee after a drone was detected near the border. articles.title: South Korean-flagged tanker attempts Hormuz crossing
articles.summary: South Korean-flagged tanker attempts Hormuz crossing A South Korean-flagged supertanker is attempting to cross the Strait of Hormuz, according to ship-tracking data cited by Bloomberg. The tanker, Universal Winner, began transmitting signals on Wednesday morning, showing its presence in the strait south of Iran’s Larak Island. Bloomberg reported that the vessel was following two Chinese supertankers that had travelled along a similar route and were also attempting to pass through the strategic waterway on Wednesday.  Vessels anchor in the Strait of Hormuz off Oman’s Musandam Peninsula, 17

articles.title: JD Vance says Iran conflict will not become 'eternal war'
articles.summary: JD Vance says Iran conflict will not become 'eternal war' US Vice President JD Vance said the conflict with Iran would not become an “eternal war”, defending President Donald Trump amid mounting pressure in Congress to limit the president’s military powers. Vance said any escalation with Tehran in the absence of a diplomatic solution would be aimed at protecting long-term US security interests. “This is not an eternal war. We will accomplish our mission and return home,” Vance said. His remarks came as lawmakers continue debating efforts to restrict Trump’s authority to pursue military action

The admiral did not respond to Smith’s questions over whether the US should take responsibility for the mistake. articles.title: Oil prices fall after Trump predicts quick end to Iran war
articles.summary: Oil prices fall after Trump predicts quick end to Iran war Oil prices fell on Wednesday after US President Donald Trump reiterated that the war with Iran would end “very quickly”, raising hopes of easing tensions in the Middle East. Brent crude futures dropped 45 cents, or 0.4 percent, to $110.83 a barrel, while US West Texas Intermediate crude futures fell 27 cents, or 0.3 percent, to $103.88. Both benchmark prices had already fallen by about $1 on Tuesday after US Vice President JD Vance said Washington and Tehran had made progress in talks and that neither side wanted to resume military act
